methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mimic Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company spun out from ETH Zurich that has raised $16M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public company (277810.KQ), though direct peer comparables at identical stage and valuation tier proved insufficient for a reliable median approach.

Given the narrow applicability of peer-median methods—the broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 peers, but none met the criteria of matching stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier simultaneously—the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. This method applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $16M total raised, reflecting typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up dynamics, yielding a point estimate of $40M.

The triangulated valuation range is $34M (low), $40M (median), to $46M (high), anchored entirely to the stage-progression approach. The wide range reflects uncertainty inherent in applying a single-method framework. Key limitations: the absence of narrow-set peer data limits confidence in cross-check validation, and stage-progression multipliers carry execution risk typical of early-stage companies. Investors should weight this valuation accordingly pending availability of more granular comparable transaction data in the robotics sector at Seed maturity.
